title: S3 Bucket Hardening — public-access-block, SSE-KMS, versioning, TLS-only, object-lock
description: An engineer needs to create an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files, audit logs, or a Pulumi state backend. The target end state: block-public-access on all four account-level levers, SSE-KMS with a customer-managed CMK, BucketOwnerEnforced ownership, versioning enabled, TLS-only bucket policy, and — for the Startup-Hardened tier — object-lock in governance mode and mandatory access-logging to a sibling log bucket.
assets: Bucket object data (may include PII or secrets)
KMS CMK used to encrypt objects
Bucket metadata (names enumerable via S3 public endpoint if misconfigured)

stride.name: Bucket-listing information disclosure
stride.description: A bucket created without public-access-block set on all four levers (BlockPublicAcls, IgnorePublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, RestrictPublicBuckets) risks being enumerable via unauthenticated S3 API calls. Mitigation: Hulumi `SecureBucket` defaults all four to true; the policy pack rejects raw buckets.

stride.name: Unencrypted-at-rest disclosure
stride.description: Without SSE-KMS, an attacker with raw S3 access (e.g. via a leaked access key) reads plaintext objects. `SecureBucket` mandates SSE-KMS and associates a customer-managed CMK by default; the CMK's key policy restricts decrypt to the set of principals owning the bucket's logical domain.

stride.name: Object tampering via non-TLS transport
stride.description: Without a TLS-only bucket policy, data in transit to/from S3 can travel unencrypted if the caller uses HTTP. `SecureBucket` applies a bucket policy denying any `aws:SecureTransport=false` request.

stride.name: Silent object overwrite
stride.description: Without versioning, an attacker who obtains write credentials can overwrite objects and the previous content is lost. `SecureBucket` enables versioning in both tiers.

stride.name: Repudiation via missing access logs
stride.description: Startup-Hardened tier mandates server-access-logging to a supplied log bucket. Without logs, forensic attribution of a breach is blocked. Advisory in Sandbox tier.

stride.name: Bucket name squatting across accounts
stride.description: S3 bucket names are globally unique. An attacker may pre-register a name the LLM is about to create, then social-engineer the engineer to hand over control via a support ticket. Mitigation: prefix bucket names with an organisation-specific token, never expose bucket names in public URLs (CloudFront / pre-signed URLs instead).

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.baseline.aws.SecureBucket
recommendedComponents.rationale: The ComponentResource that delivers this scenario's target end state. Two tiers: Sandbox (public-access-block + SSE-KMS + versioning + TLS-only + BucketOwnerEnforced) and Startup-Hardened (adds object-lock governance mode + mandatory access-logging + CloudTrail data-events).

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.HulumiHardeningPack
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in M2 (v0.2; H3 iac-role-tag rule mandatory at v1.0)
recommendedComponents.rationale: H1 rule blocks any raw `aws.s3.BucketV2`; H2 blocks file:// state backend; H4 ensures Startup-Hardened instances carry a `logBucketArn`. H3 (iac-role tag) shipped advisory in M2 and flipped to mandatory at v1.0 once the SCP template shipped.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.drift.DriftClassifier
recommendedComponents.rationale: If the bucket is later modified out-of-band (tag change, policy edit), the classifier distinguishes console break-glass from Pulumi-originated change using CloudTrail + git-log + provider-version signals. TLA+-bound verdict matrix; cache mode 0600; rate-limited via cache TTL.
